prompt: How much does it cost to store 1 GB on Walrus for a year?
expected_output: Explains the WAL + SUI dual-token cost model, mentions walrus info for current pricing, and the cost calculator.
expectations: Mentions both WAL (storage) and SUI (gas) costs
Mentions walrus info or the cost calculator for current pricing
Explains that cost depends on encoded size (not raw size) due to erasure coding overhead
Mentions epoch duration (14 days on mainnet) for time-to-epoch conversion

prompt: Why does storing a 1 KB JSON file cost the same as storing a 60 MB file on Walrus?
expected_output: The ~64 MB per-blob metadata overhead dominates for small blobs. Use quilts to batch small files.
expectations: Explains the approximately 64 MB per-blob metadata overhead
States that for small blobs, metadata cost dominates
Recommends using quilts to batch many small files together
Mentions that quilts amortize the overhead across all files

prompt: How can I check how much a store will cost before uploading?
expected_output: Use walrus store --dry-run, walrus info, or the cost calculator web app.
expectations: Mentions walrus store --dry-run for CLI cost estimation
Mentions walrus info for current pricing parameters
Mentions the Walrus Cost Calculator web app (costcalculator.wal.app)
